title: phaeneilema malabaricum
intro: Phaeneilema malabaricum, also known as Indian kidney bean or Malabar kidney bean, is a plant species that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Its leaves, roots, and stems are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders, and are also said to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties, providing health benefits like improved immune function and reduced inflammation. The herbal preparations of Phaeneilema malabaricum include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally consumed as teas or added to food to harness its medicinal properties.
